Summary: The webpage provides a gunfire map for Berkeley in 2026, detailing shooting incidents. As of April 9, there has been one injury shooting and five non-injury shootings. The map distinguishes between injury and non-injury shootings and shows their locations within Berkeley City Council districts. The article mentions Berkeley police, UC police, and the Oakland Police Department, and notes that the map is a resource for residents to stay informed about crime and safety in their neighborhoods.

A deceased man was discovered in bushes near Sproul Hall on the UC Berkeley campus. Authorities do not believe the man was affiliated with the university and found no obvious signs of foul play. The Alameda County coroner's office will determine the cause of death, and the man's identity has not yet been released. UC Berkeley is offering counseling services to its students, including after-hours and drop-in options.

A UC Berkeley student, William Yi Lu, 21, majoring in Theater & Performance Studies, died from a fall outside a campus housing complex. Authorities are investigating the incident, which occurred on Durant Avenue. The University of California Police Department confirmed there is no known threat to the community. Counseling services are available to students affected by this tragedy.

A UC Berkeley student, William Yi Lu, 21, majoring in Theater & Performance Studies, died from a fall outside a student housing complex. Authorities are investigating the incident, which occurred on Durant Avenue at the Unit 3 housing complex. The University of California Police Department confirmed there is no known threat to the community. Counseling services are available to students affected by this tragedy.

Two individuals were discovered deceased in a car at Berkeley's Aquatic Park on Sunday. Early reports suggest a possible overdose, with no clear signs of trauma at the scene. Volunteers performing a trash cleanup alerted authorities after noticing the pair in the disabled vehicle. The Berkeley Police Department is investigating the deaths, while the coroner's office will determine the cause.

Two people were found deceased in a car at Berkeley's Aquatic Park over the weekend. Early reports suggest a possible overdose, with no clear signs of trauma. Volunteers alerted authorities after noticing the pair in a disabled parking spot. The Alameda County coroner's office is investigating the cause of death, and the identities of the deceased are currently unavailable.

This webpage is an op-ed by Amory Langmo, president of the Berkeley Fire Fighters Association. Langmo argues against proposed cuts to the Fire Department's staffing and the closure of Fire Station 4. He highlights that citizens have twice voted to fund the department, providing a clear mandate that the proposed cuts ignore. Langmo urges the city to reject these cuts and find alternative solutions, emphasizing that the city is growing while the Fire Department would be moving backward.

This webpage is an op-ed by Amory Langmo, president of the Berkeley Fire Fighters Association. The main topic is the proposed cuts to the Berkeley Fire Department's staffing and the closure of Fire Station 4. Langmo argues that these cuts contradict the public's mandate, demonstrated by two tax measures, to fund public safety services. He highlights that the city has grown significantly while these proposed cuts would effectively roll back the department's resources by 20 years.

A driver, Hector Juarez-Perez, has been arrested for a felony hit-and-run in Berkeley. He struck a pedestrian, a man in his 20s, who was carried on his hood for 125 feet before falling. The pedestrian sustained serious head and body injuries. Police are still searching for the suspect's black 2014 Toyota Camry.

A driver, Hector Juarez-Perez, has been arrested for a felony hit-and-run in Berkeley. He struck a pedestrian, a man in his 20s, and carried him on his hood for 125 feet before fleeing the scene. The pedestrian sustained serious head and body injuries. Police are still searching for the suspect's vehicle, a black 2014 Toyota Camry.

A man in his 50s riding an e-bike was hospitalized after a crash with a car at Cedar Street and San Pablo Avenue in Berkeley. The rider, who was not wearing a helmet, went over his handlebars and sustained visible injuries. He was transported to Highland Hospital by ambulance with lights and sirens. The article mentions e-bike riders, car drivers, and pedestrians as social groups involved in or affected by traffic incidents.

The rider was conscious at the scene but was transported to Highland Hospital "Code 3." Authorities noted that the e-bike rider was not wearing a helmet.

The webpage is a collection of recent news headlines from Berkeley, California. The main topics covered are crime, UC Berkeley incidents, and traffic safety. Key words include "arrest," "crash," "shooting," "deceased," and "charges." Social groups mentioned include students, pedestrians, e-bike/scooter riders, and victims of elder abuse.
